title: The Boolean Type
description: Write logic using true or false values.

slides.title: On or Off 🔘
slides.content:
A boolean is the simplest type of data. It literally only has two possible values: `true` or `false`.

Booleans are typically used to turn features on or off, or heavily used in `if/else` statements.

slides.title: Declaring Booleans
slides.codeSnippet:
let isGameOver: boolean = false;
let isWinner: boolean = true;

quizData.question: Which of these is a valid boolean value?
quizData.explanation: Booleans must be exactly `true` or `false` (no quotes).

slides.title: Comparisons -> Booleans ⚖️
slides.content: Every time you use comparison operators (like `<`, `>`, `===`), the result is *always* evaluated as a boolean under the hood.

slides.title: Assigning from Logic
slides.content: You can assign boolean variables based on logical checks:
